response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned web and full-stack engineer with deep Node.js/TypeScript expertise and demonstrable high-throughput distributed systems experience, but this role is fundamentally a C++ systems engineering position for industrial automation—a domain where the candidate has minimal and heavily dated exposure. His only professional C++ work spans approximately six months at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no evidence of STL-heavy development, concurrent systems architecture, or performance-critical embedded work in C++. There is no background in IEC 62443 secure development lifecycles, MQTT, industrial protocols, or IoT platforms, making the overall profile misaligned with the core technical requirements of the role.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, event-driven distributed systems (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and low-latency architecture)
Broad polyglot background across DLang, Java, C#, TypeScript, and others, suggesting capacity to work in unfamiliar languages
Strong agile engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development, with active knowledge-sharing via workshops and open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old—approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015—with no evidence of modern C++ standards, STL proficiency, or multi-threaded systems design in C++
No industrial automation, IoT, or industrial protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP3); the candidate's entire domain is web and cloud software
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) or IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, both of which are explicit hard requirements for this role

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IoT domain knowledge
